items.title: Londoners react to King Charles’ state visit invite to Trump
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>After U.S. President Donald Trump received a formal invitation from King Charles for an unprecedented second state visit, an online petition calling to the invitation to be revoked gathered more than 145 thousand signatures.
 
#Reuters #News #KingCharles #donaldtrump #Londoners

items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.title: Car rams into crowd in Germany's Mannheim, killing at least two | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>A car drove into a crowd in the German city of Mannheim, killing at least two people and seriously injuring several others, overshadowing carnival celebrations in the region.

items.title: Pope Francis, the Argentine pontiff who never returned home | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>‘He is very Argentine’: Pope Francis has not visited his home country since he became the head of the Roman Catholic Church in 2013. Some say it is due to the polarized political situation there.

items.title: LIVE: US court to hear arguments over consumer bureau lawsuit
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>The US District Court will hear arguments on a lawsuit challenging the Trump administration's efforts to significantly curtail the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. The lawsuit was brought by a federal employee union against Trump administration officials, challenging aggressive efforts to halt agency work and remove staff.
 
#us #consumerbureau #lawsuit #trumpadministration #federalemployeeunion #live #news #reuters

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.title: Sectors Up Close: Utilities 'offer defense against geopolitical risk' | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>As market sentiment gets more risk-averse, defensive stocks have been rising - with the S&P 500 utilities sector more than 4% up on the year. Matt Miskin of John Hancock Investment Management told Reuters utility companies provide a hedge against rising prices.

items.title: Market Talk: Markets are 'confident' governments will borrow more for defence | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>European defense firms saw their shares jump. There are big questions over how European governments plan to pay for a major increase in defense spending — but Janet Mui of RBC Brewin Dolphin told Reuters the market is pricing in a hike in public sector borrowing.
